core_mechanism: noise-induced regularization: adding a multiplicative, transport-type stochastic perturbation to a nonlinear evolution equation prone to finite-time blow-up, so that the random forcing suppresses singularity formation and yields globally well-behaved (smooth) solutions with high probability
brief_reason: The abstract states that a suitably chosen transport noise driving the 3D Navier-Stokes equations produces global-in-time smooth solutions with high probability, i.e. a stochastic perturbation regularizes an otherwise potentially-singular nonlinear PDE uniformly over large initial data.
